title: The One With The Yeti

jokes.text: Hey, hey, hey, hey. None of that. Not while you're living under my roof.
jokes.setup: Joey walks in on his roommates being romantic
jokes.punchline: His overly parental reaction to adults being affectionate
jokes.explanation: Strong character moment - Joey's childlike discomfort with sexuality is perfectly in character and creates genuine awkwardness

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa! What is going on here?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You two need to stop that right now. There are rules in this apartment.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You're not gonna sit here and make out on my couch like a couple of teenagers.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: If you're gonna do that, at least have the decency to go to a bedroom or something.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I mean, come on! Show some respect!

jokes.text: Well, I'm sorry if I'm not a middle-aged black woman.
jokes.setup: Question about being late from an audition
jokes.punchline: Reveals he went to completely wrong audition for different demographic
jokes.explanation: Excellent Joey moment - perfectly captures his acting incompetence with unexpected specificity. The randomness is earned through character history.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I went to the wrong audition.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, I showed up and they were looking for someone completely different. Like, they wanted this old guy, and I'm standing there like, 'Hi, I'm here for the role!' And they're like, 'We need someone who collects social security.'

jokes.text: And I'm also sorry if sometimes I go to the wrong audition.
jokes.setup: Previous line about wrong audition
jokes.punchline: Doubling down with 'sometimes' suggesting this happens regularly
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that reinforces Joey's incompetence, though less punchy than the initial reveal

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Well, sometimes I go to the wrong audition.

jokes.text: I can hear that.
jokes.setup: Couple presumably making out after his warning
jokes.punchline: His deadpan acknowledgment that they're ignoring his request
jokes.explanation: Solid callback with good economy - the minimalist delivery sells Joey's resignation

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, hey, hey! I said no making out in my apartment!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You know what? Forget it. I can see you're not listening to me.

jokes.text: I can still hear you.
jokes.setup: Continuation of the 'I can hear that' bit
jokes.punchline: Escalates his discomfort by emphasizing they haven't stopped
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation but predictable - still works because of Joey's delivery style

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I can hear that! I can hear that! Why haven't you stopped?!

jokes.text: A year ago I didn't even have a family and now I have heirlooms for crying out loud.
jokes.setup: Phoebe showing off family heirloom
jokes.punchline: Her amazement at going from no family to having fancy inherited items
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment that captures Phoebe's journey from street kid to having family connections

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Look at this! It's a Limoges box. My grandmother left it to me. Can you believe it? Me! With a family heirloom!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I went from living on the street with nothing to having fancy inherited items. I'm like a real person now!

jokes.text: Oh, God! Aah!
jokes.setup: Someone touches the fur coat
jokes.punchline: Startled screaming reaction suggesting unexpected texture
jokes.explanation: Basic physical comedy reaction - functional but not particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa! What is that?!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: It's a fur coat!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh my God, it's so soft!

jokes.text: Oh, soft.
jokes.setup: His scream of terror from touching the coat
jokes.punchline: Immediately switches to appreciating its softness
jokes.explanation: Excellent comedic timing - the instant mood shift from terror to appreciation is perfectly Joey

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Ahhhh! Get it off me!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait... this is really soft.

jokes.text: Yeah, just some 9-year-old Filipino kids who worked their fingers bloody for 12 cents an hour.
jokes.setup: Phoebe's pride about her cruelty-free coat
jokes.punchline: Ross pointing out the human cost of her 'ethical' alternative
jokes.explanation: Sharp dark humor that subverts Phoebe's moral superiority - unexpected edge for Ross but well-executed

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Wait, where'd you get that coat? It's nice.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Thanks! It's completely cruelty-free. No animals were harmed.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yeah, but what about the workers in the synthetic fabric factory in Southeast Asia working 14-hour days for 40 cents an hour?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: ...I didn't think about that.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: So basically, you're just being cruel to people instead of animals.

jokes.text: That didn't happen, I made that up.
jokes.setup: His detailed description of child labor
jokes.punchline: Casually admitting he fabricated the disturbing details
jokes.explanation: Nice deflation of the dark moment - shows Ross recognizing he went too far

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Actually, I made all that up.

jokes.text: So basically this is a 'getting rid of anything Rachel ever touched' sale.
jokes.setup: Ross explaining why he's selling all his furniture
jokes.punchline: Monica cutting through his euphemisms to the real reason
jokes.explanation: Monica's directness cuts through Ross's self-deception perfectly - economical and character-driven

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I'm just ready for a change, you know? Time to move on to a new chapter, embrace minimalism...

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You can't afford the furniture payments.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: ...Yes.

jokes.text: Touched, used, sat on, slept on... I'll take it all.
jokes.setup: Monica listing what Rachel did to the furniture
jokes.punchline: Joey's enthusiastic interest in Rachel-contaminated items
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - his attraction to Rachel makes this both creepy and endearing

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: She put her feet all over the couch.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: She put her feet on the couch?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: And she ate cookies in bed.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Cookies in bed?!

jokes.text: When my friend Sylvie's husband said someone else's name in bed, she cursed him and turned his thingy green.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Ross saying Rachel's name at his wedding
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's tale of supernatural revenge involving genital discoloration
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe - combines her mystical beliefs with crude humor and uses 'thingy' to make it simultaneously childish and adult

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh, that happened to my ex-boyfriend Eric. He said another girl's name at his wedding, and that very night, his thing turned blue.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: His thing turned blue?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Yeah, it was the voodoo. His new wife, she was Haitian.

jokes.text: Emily thinks Ross' furniture has got Rachel cooties?
jokes.setup: The adults discussing Ross getting rid of Rachel-touched furniture
jokes.punchline: Joey reducing it to playground-level 'cooties' concept
jokes.explanation: Joey's childlike perspective perfectly captures the absurdity of Emily's demands

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait. You're throwing out the couch because Rachel sat on it?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: We're not throwing it out, we're just—

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: That's like saying a girl has cooties! You can't use the couch now because Rachel cooties are on it!

jokes.text: Yeah, I know. We're losing Ross, Joey said 'hence.'
jokes.setup: Joey expressing concern about losing Ross and using sophisticated vocabulary
jokes.punchline: Chandler being equally disturbed by Joey's uncharacteristic word choice
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Chandler observation - treating Joey's vocabulary as equally concerning as losing Ross is perfect character work with great economy

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I'm really perturbed about losing Ross. It's a travesty of monumental proportions.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Okay, now I'm scared. Not about losing Ross — about whatever alien abducted Joey and replaced him with someone who uses the word 'perturbed.'

jokes.text: But, you know, I'm an actor. I'll act cool.
jokes.setup: Discussion about hiding feelings about Emily
jokes.punchline: Joey's literal interpretation of 'acting cool' as using his acting skills
jokes.explanation: Solid Joey moment showing his literal mindset, though the wordplay is fairly obvious

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You gotta act cool, man. Just pretend you don't care.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I can't just act cool, Joey. I'm terrible at this stuff.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: No, no, no. You're an actor. Well, I'm an actor. You know what I mean. Just use your acting skills!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Watch me. Hey Emily, how ya doin'?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: See? Cool. I'm acting. I don't even like you that much.

jokes.text: I wonder if the best place to put something that cooks food is underneath the poison?
jokes.setup: Finding waffle iron stored under bug bomb
jokes.punchline: Her sarcastic questioning of the storage logic
jokes.explanation: Good Monica moment showcasing her practical nature and food safety concerns

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: A waffle iron under a bug bomb? Really? Because that's exactly where I keep my small appliances—right next to toxic chemicals. Why not throw it in with the rat poison while we're at it?

jokes.text: Fog him! Fog him!
jokes.setup: Encountering the 'yeti' in storage
jokes.punchline: Military-style commands for using bug spray as weapon
jokes.explanation: Decent physical comedy moment - the urgency of 'fog him' as tactical command is amusing

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Okay, everyone stay calm. We need to approach this strategically.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: What do you mean 'strategically'? It's a bug!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Not just any bug. This is a full-scale invasion. Chandler, get the bug spray. NOW!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yes, ma'am!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Listen up, people. When I give the signal, I want a two-pronged attack. Joey, you come in from the left flank. Phoebe, cover the rear exit.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Monica, it's literally the size of a pea.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Size is irrelevant, Rachel. It's the principle of the thing. Chandler, spray pattern: short bursts, three-second intervals. Do you understand?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I understand, Colonel Monica.

jokes.text: You're on in five, Ms. Minnelli.
jokes.setup: Monica trying on the fur coat
jokes.punchline: Joey comparing her to Liza Minnelli in glamorous fur
jokes.explanation: Serviceable pop culture reference - works for the era but fairly predictable celebrity comparison

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh my God, Monica, you look just like Liza Minnelli!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Really? You think so?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah! If Liza Minnelli was a crazy cat lady.

jokes.text: I've been given the gift of time.
jokes.setup: Ross explaining his long commute as a positive
jokes.punchline: Spinning obvious inconvenience as profound gift
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ross's desperate self-deception with pretentious language

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: You know, I've been thinking about my commute, and it's actually not so bad. It gives me time to think, to reflect on life's bigger questions.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Plus, all that walking? That's basically a gym membership. I'm getting exercise, fresh air, and personal growth all at the same time.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Really, when you think about it, my commute isn't a burden — it's a gift. A beautiful gift that the universe is giving me every single day.

jokes.text: That's so funny, because last Christmas I got the gift of space. We should get them together and make a continuum.
jokes.setup: Ross's 'gift of time' comment
jokes.punchline: Chandler referencing his breakup as 'gift of space' and suggesting combining them scientifically
jokes.explanation: Excellent Chandler wit - combines self-deprecation with nerdy space-time reference while mocking Ross's forced optimism

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, if time is a gift, and you're giving her space, maybe we should combine them scientifically and see if we can create some kind of time-space continuum that gets me away from my relationship problems.

jokes.text: He was like this crazy-eyed, hairy beast-man. He was like a Bigfoot or a yeti or something.
jokes.setup: Describing the scary man they encountered
jokes.punchline: Escalating descriptions culminating in mythical creature comparisons
jokes.explanation: Sets up the yeti misunderstanding well but fairly standard escalating description

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: He was huge! Like, really big.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: How big?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Like a bear. No, bigger than a bear. Like a Bigfoot.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Bigfoot? Monica, come on.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I'm telling you, he was like a mythical creature. Like a troll or something.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: A troll? In New York?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I don't know! He was terrifying! He could've been a dragon for all I know!

jokes.text: Yeah, I pulled the tab and I just fogged his yeti ass.
jokes.setup: Description of using bug spray on the 'creature'
jokes.punchline: Rachel's proud, crude description of her bug spray attack
jokes.explanation: Good Rachel moment - her pride in the attack and crude language work well together

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I sprayed it with bug spray until it stopped moving. I think I killed it.

jokes.text: Ugh, please. We did not fog Danny.
jokes.setup: Gunther revealing they attacked their new neighbor Danny
jokes.punchline: Rachel's indignant denial despite just bragging about it
jokes.explanation: Nice immediate contradiction showing Rachel's embarrassment at learning the truth

dialogue.speaker: Gunther
dialogue.line: We attacked our new neighbor Danny.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: We did not attack him! We just... we went over there and we told him what we thought of his stupid fence!

dialogue.speaker: Gunther
dialogue.line: You pushed him into the bushes.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: That was an accident!

jokes.text: You know, you always stick up for the people we fog.
jokes.setup: Gunther defending Danny
jokes.punchline: Rachel acting like they regularly fog people and Gunther always defends the victims
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist escalation - treating one incident as pattern and making Gunther the serial fog-victim defender

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Wait, so you're saying the exterminator was just doing his job? He only gassed like five people!

dialogue.speaker: Gunther
dialogue.line: It was an accident. He felt terrible about it.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Gunther, this is the third time this month! Last week he fogged the entire second floor. The week before, he cleared out a wedding reception.

dialogue.speaker: Gunther
dialogue.line: He's a good man, Rachel. He apologized each time.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Apologized?! Gunther, people were hospitalized! And you always take his side!

jokes.text: We actually thought you were like a yeti or something. Okay.
jokes.setup: Monica explaining their mistake to Danny
jokes.punchline: Danny's completely flat, unforgiving 'Okay' response
jokes.explanation: Great cringe comedy - Danny's minimal, cold response makes the girls' elaborate explanation seem pathetic

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I know we messed up. We should have told you sooner.

dialogue.speaker: Danny
dialogue.line: Okay.

jokes.text: I don't think that we can accept your acceptance of our apology. It just doesn't really seem like you mean it.
jokes.setup: Danny's cold 'Okay' responses to their apologies
jokes.punchline: Rachel's convoluted logic rejecting his acceptance for being insufficient
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist logic - the circular reasoning about apology acceptance is genuinely funny wordplay

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait. You can't just say 'okay' like that. If you really accepted our apology, you would have said it with more enthusiasm. The fact that you said it so coldly means you didn't actually accept it, which means we still need to apologize!

jokes.text: Really. What is with that guy? I mean, you'd forgive me if I fogged you. Well, you did, a little bit.
jokes.setup: Monica complaining about Danny being rude
jokes.punchline: Rachel casually admitting she also fogged Monica during the incident
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that adds friendly fire element to the chaos - Monica's casual forgiveness contrasts with Danny

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Danny was so rude to me today!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, I fogged you too.

jokes.text: Well, he said that he would cremate my fur coat for free if I, um, you know, bring in the next person I know who dies.
jokes.setup: Phoebe explaining Crematorium Chris's offer
jokes.punchline: The morbid quid pro quo arrangement treating death casually
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark Phoebe humor - the casual arrangement with death and her matter-of-fact delivery is perfect

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Okay, so Crematorium Chris said that if I sleep with him, he'll cremate my parents for free.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Phoebe, that's insane!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I know, right? It's like, either way I'm getting screwed... and someone's getting burned.

jokes.text: Not nature. Fashion.
jokes.setup: Phoebe saying destroying the coat is crime against nature
jokes.punchline: Rachel correcting that it's actually about fashion, not nature
jokes.explanation: Solid Rachel moment showing her fashion priorities - simple but character-appropriate correction

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Destroying that coat is a crime against nature.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Actually, Phoebe, it's a crime against fashion.

jokes.text: Okay, so to you, death is fashion? That's really funny. Yeah, here's Phoebe, uh, sporting, you know, cutting-edge hairy carcass from, you know, the steel traps of wintry Russia.
jokes.setup: Rachel's fashion argument
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's sarcastic fashion commentary with graphic imagery
jokes.explanation: Good Phoebe moral stance with vivid imagery - slightly verbose but captures her passion

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh, I'm sorry, I didn't realize that wearing a dead animal had to make a fashion statement. I mean, I thought it was just tacky.

jokes.text: Because I do.
jokes.setup: Her passionate anti-fur speech asking if Rachel thinks it looks good
jokes.punchline: Phoebe admitting she actually does think it looks good
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - Phoebe completely undermining her moral stance with simple honesty. Excellent economy and character work.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I mean, look at this. How can you wear the skin of an innocent creature? Don't you think that's wrong?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well, yeah, I guess...

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I mean, it looks really good on you though.

jokes.text: So rocks.
jokes.setup: Ross's elaborate description of 'fossil samples'
jokes.punchline: Joey's reductive summary cutting through pretension
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - his simple truth cuts through Ross's academic pretension with great economy

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: So you're saying... rocks?

jokes.text: We all hate Emily.
jokes.setup: Ross asking what's going on with their behavior
jokes.punchline: Joey's blunt, immediate honesty breaking their pretense
jokes.explanation: Excellent Joey moment - his inability to maintain pretense creates perfect uncomfortable truth-telling

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What is going on with you two?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: We're not doing anything.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yes, you are. You're acting weird.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: No, we're not.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Joey, come on.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Fine! We're sleeping together, okay? There, you happy now?

jokes.text: You guys hang out at the coffeehouse way too much.
jokes.setup: Ross's speech about real adult life vs hanging at Central Perk
jokes.punchline: Joey taking the wrong lesson and focusing on location frequency
jokes.explanation: Great meta moment - Joey missing the point while accidentally critiquing the show's premise

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: You know, I've been thinking about it, and we really should be focusing on our careers and our futures instead of spending all our time at Central Perk.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, yeah, you're right. So basically, we need to go to Central Perk more often so people know where to find us.

jokes.text: Well, ahem, I've been reading up, and for your information, minks are not very nice.
jokes.setup: Monica questioning Phoebe's animal rights consistency
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's childlike moral justification based on personality
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe logic - applying moral judgments based on animal personalities rather than species protection

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Wait, you eat meat? I thought you were a vegetarian.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I am! I don't eat red meat.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: But you just ordered a steak.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Yeah, but it's a cow. Cows are mean.

jokes.text: It's the best thing I've ever had wrapped around me, including Phil Huntley. Remember Phil Huntley? He was fine.
jokes.setup: Phoebe admitting she loves the coat
jokes.punchline: Comparing coat favorably to past lover, then rating the guy
jokes.explanation: Good Phoebe overshare with nice escalation from coat praise to sexual comparison to nostalgic rating

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I love this coat! It's so soft and warm.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: You know, it's better than my last boyfriend. At least this coat doesn't cheat on me and it actually keeps me warm at night.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I'd give the guy a 3 out of 10, but the coat? Solid 9.

jokes.text: Absolutely. Some people are just into appearances.
jokes.setup: Rachel apologizing for judging him on appearance
jokes.punchline: Danny sarcastically agreeing while clearly judging her shopping-obsessed appearance
jokes.explanation: Solid sarcastic comeback that reveals Danny's own prejudices while seeming to agree

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I'm sorry for judging you on your appearance.

dialogue.speaker: Danny
dialogue.line: Oh, that's okay. I don't judge people on their appearance. I mean, look at you - you're clearly just a natural, down-to-earth person who has no interest in shopping or designer labels whatsoever.

jokes.text: You know, I could have toys for underprivileged kids in here. Do you? Well, you know, if kids like to play with capri pants.
jokes.setup: Rachel defending that her bags could contain charitable items
jokes.punchline: Her immediate admission that it's just clothes, making a weak justification
jokes.explanation: Good setup-punchline with Rachel's defensive lie immediately deflated by reality

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: They could be for charity!

dialogue.speaker: Danny
dialogue.line: Really?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Okay, fine, it's clothes. But I'm going to wear them, and then I'm going to donate them!

jokes.text: And stop saying that. I hate that. Okay.
jokes.setup: Rachel's frustration with Danny's flat responses
jokes.punchline: Danny immediately doing the thing she just said she hates
jokes.explanation: Good running gag payoff - Danny's immediate repetition of the annoying behavior escalates the frustration

dialogue.line: Danny immediately doing the thing she just said she hates

jokes.text: That pizza place across the street any good? What? I'm hungry. You wanna get some pizza? You can keep yelling if there's more.
jokes.setup: Rachel's passionate speech about snap judgments
jokes.punchline: Danny completely ignoring her point to focus on food, offering to let her continue ranting
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - Danny's casual dismissal of her speech while being oddly accommodating about her anger

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: You can't just make snap judgments about people! It's unfair and it's shallow and it's exactly the kind of thing that I hate!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, you want a sandwich? I'm gonna make a sandwich.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Joey! Did you hear what I just said?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, yeah, no snap judgments, very deep. You want turkey or roast beef?

jokes.text: It's not like I'm wearing a seeing-eye dog coat.
jokes.setup: Friends criticizing her for wearing fur
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's absurd comparison to an even more morally problematic coat
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Phoebe logic - creating an even worse scenario to make her current choice seem reasonable. Dark, original, and perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: What? It's not real fur!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Phoebe, that's a dead animal.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: It's a dead animal, but it's not real fur. There's a difference.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: No, there really isn't.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Well, I think it's fine. I mean, we eat chicken, right? Would you wear a chicken?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: No.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Well, what if it was a really fancy chicken?

jokes.text: That yeti is one smooth talker.
jokes.setup: Rachel going to pizza with Danny after their conflict
jokes.punchline: Monica sarcastically crediting Danny's charm despite his minimal responses
jokes.explanation: Good callback that ironically contrasts Danny's actual communication style with successful dating

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh wow, Danny's charm really shines through. I mean, he said 'yeah' and 'okay.' That's basically poetry.

jokes.text: You see, Rach, I'm an actor.
jokes.setup: Joey's emotional outburst about Ross leaving
jokes.punchline: His explanation defaulting to his standard 'I'm an actor' excuse
jokes.explanation: Decent callback to Joey's tendency to blame his profession for his behavior - predictable but consistent

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I can't believe Ross is leaving! This is the worst day of my life!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I'm an actor.

jokes.text: Oh, God. I really missed that fat bastard.
jokes.setup: Seeing Ugly Naked Guy again
jokes.punchline: Ross's surprisingly crude but affectionate description
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ross's unexpected crudeness mixed with genuine affection

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Oh my God, he's alive! I was so worried about him. I mean, he hasn't moved in like three days.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: But look at him now, just sitting there... beautiful son of a bitch.

jokes.text: Well, I can tell you for damn sure who's not here, and that's Rachel.
jokes.setup: Emily asking who's at dinner via speakerphone with Rachel right there
jokes.punchline: Joey's emphatic lie while Rachel is visibly present
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - Joey's overemphatic denial while the evidence contradicts him creates perfect awkwardness

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: No, no, no, Rachel's not here!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: *visible in background*

jokes.text: You can totally tell. Here, look, watch me. Smile. Frown. Smile. Frown. Smile.
jokes.setup: Debate about whether you can tell emotions from back of head
jokes.punchline: Joey's physical demonstration with exaggerated facial expressions no one can see
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy moment showing Joey's literal interpretation and commitment to proving his point

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: No, no, no, you can totally tell what someone's feeling just by looking at the back of their head. See? Right now I'm doing happy.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: And now I'm doing sad. Now I'm doing confused. Now I'm doing sexy.

jokes.text: This is mink, okay? They're mean, and they hate squirrels.
jokes.setup: Squirrel seemingly judging her for wearing fur
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's defensive explanation to the animal about species rivalry
jokes.explanation: Good Phoebe moment - her ability to talk to animals while justifying wearing them shows perfect character contradiction

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh, don't look at me like that! You know what? We're not even the same species. This is inter-species rivalry. It's not fair!

jokes.text: And, you know, most of these probably wanted to be coats.
jokes.setup: Her defensive explanation about minks being mean
jokes.punchline: Escalating to suggesting the animals had career aspirations
jokes.explanation: Excellent Phoebe absurdism - the idea of minks having fashion career goals is brilliantly ridiculous and perfectly delivered

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Well, minks are mean! They're mean little creatures. They have those beady little eyes and they're always looking for a way to bite you.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Plus, they probably wanted to be on TV anyway. I mean, they're minks! They had dreams of being in movies, you know? They probably resented being trapped in a lab when they could've been auditioning for fur coat commercials.

tmdb_overview: Emily decides that the only way to continue her marriage with Ross is to have him get rid of all traces of Rachel in his apartment.  Meanwhile, Rachel and Monica mistake a hairy neighbor for a yeti.
insight: Just-below-elite 79 leans on character work and escalation over standout zingers.
